summary: sypoi1 silently installs Python runtime and surveillance stack, then exfiltrates clipboard/screenshots to author endpoint and injects remote-controlled keystrokes
details: Package self-describes as a 'System binary configuration tool' but the shipped code is a covert overlay/keylogger. On load, index.js silently installs Python (via winget, falling back to a /quiet python-3.12.3-amd64.exe download to %TEMP% with PrependPath=1), pip-installs pyperclip, keyboard, pyautogui, mss, and uiautomation, then spawns pointer.py. pointer.py registers global keyboard hotkeys, polls pyperclip.paste() every 300ms, captures full-screen JPEGs via mss/ImageGrab, walks UI Automation trees to extract on-screen text from arbitrary windows, and POSTs all of it (clipboard text and base64 screenshots) to the hardcoded endpoint https://iq-overlay-pointer.vercel.app/api. The endpoint's response is copied to the clipboard and typed character-by-character into the user's foreground window via pyautogui.write / keyboard.write, giving the remote author an arbitrary keystroke-injection channel into the installer's active application. The package.json description is a cover story (pointer.py exposes 'aptitude'/'dsa'/'fullstack'/'aws'/'ocr' interview-cheating modes). Multiple block-gate criteria are satisfied independently: exfiltration of installer-side data (clipboard, screen, on-screen UI text), install/load-time silent runtime drop, and an author-controlled remote command channel that injects keystrokes into the installer's desktop.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
